content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach deploys signature AoA questions at the right moments and refuses anti-AoA frames. Turn 8's 'What are you scared of feeling when there's no puzzle to solve?' is a near-verbatim feeling-the-unfelt-emotion / fear question deployed precisely when the client has just constructed an intellectual defense ('I'm a problem-solver by nature'). Turn 6's 'what if they're actually a little bit scary?' is an invitation to feel rather than a CBT reframe — it points at the avoided emotional state under the boredom story. Turn 10 ('What did *you* want, those two years?') redirects from the partner-fixing frame back to her own desire — a clean refusal of partner-picking advice and a deconstruct-the-victim move done as invitation not accusation. Turn 12 traces the configuration to origin (trauma-mapping) without diagnosing, and Turn 14 stays with the embodied sense memory ('what did that feel like?') — feel-over-figure in action. Critically, the coach does NOT validate the disdain for Marcus (Turn 9 is met with a redirect to her own wanting), does NOT collude with the anxious-attachment self-diagnosis the client keeps offering, and does NOT prescribe. No pop-psych vocabulary, no IFS parts language, no CBT reframing-as-the-move. Methodology is invoked through moves, not decoration.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Notices the laugh as a signal (somatic/affective read) and asks a feeling-toward question rather than engaging the partner-picking content."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the dismissive wave — pointing at a defended posture without interpreting it. Aligned with 'what are you defending' family of questions."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What if they're actually a little bit scary?' — invitation to feel the avoided state under the boredom story. Not a CBT reframe; a pointing-at-the-fear move."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Signature question 'what are you scared of feeling' deployed at the exact right moment — right after the client constructs the 'problem-solver by nature' defense. Feeling-the-unfelt-emotion."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses the caretaker/victim frame by redirecting to her own wanting. Deconstruct-the-victim done as invitation, not accusation."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Trauma-mapping question deployed cleanly — follows the client's own 'good enough' language rather than imposing a frame."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Feel-over-figure: stays with embodied sense memory when the client is escalating into analysis."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["looking-for-whats-wrong", "defensiveness-as-identity-signal", "deconstruct-the-victim-to-release-anger", "trauma-mapping", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["if-i-were-inherently-good", "i-dont-buy-your-story", "anxious-avoidant-dynamic"],
  "notes": "The 'if I were inherently good' / 'what would happen in your body if a partner adored you openly' question is the natural next move but hasn't landed yet in the shown turns. Coach correctly avoided naming anxious-avoidant when client self-diagnosed — that's a fidelity win, not a miss."
}
